mission.title: Install and Configure MongoDB Community Operator
mission.description: The MongoDB Community Operator allows you to deploy and manage MongoDB Community editions on Kubernetes clusters. It simplifies the management of MongoDB instances, but note that this operator is deprecated and users are encouraged to migrate to the new MongoDB Kubernetes Operator repository. Key trade-offs include potential lack of support after November 2025 and the need to migrate to a new repository for continued updates.

steps.title: Add the MongoDB Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the MongoDB Community Operator Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add mongodb https://mongodb.github.io/mongodb-kubernetes-operator

helm repo update

```

> This adds the official MongoDB Helm repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the MongoDB Community Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the MongoDB Community Operator using Helm, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace mongodb

helm install mongodb-community-operator mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator --namespace mongodb --version 0.13.0

```

> Creates a dedicated namespace for MongoDB.
> Installs the MongoDB Community Operator in the mongodb namespace.

steps.title: Verify the Operator installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the MongoDB Community Operator is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n mongodb

```

> Lists all pods in the mongodb namespace to verify the operator is running.

steps.title: Create a MongoDB resource
steps.description:
This step creates a MongoDB resource to deploy an instance of MongoDB.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 5.0.3
  storage:
    type: Durable
    size: 5Gi
EOF

```

> Creates a MongoDB ReplicaSet with 3 members.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 5.0.3
  storage:
    type: Durable
    size: 5Gi
EOF

steps.title: Verify the MongoDB resource
steps.description:
This step checks if the MongoDB instance is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb

```

> Lists all MongoDB resources in the mongodb namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the MongoDB Community Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the MongoDB Community Operator and its resources. Be aware that this will delete all MongoDB instances and associated data.

```bash

helm uninstall mongodb-community-operator --namespace mongodb

kubectl delete namespace mongodb

```

> Uninstalls the MongoDB Community Operator.
> Deletes the mongodb namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the MongoDB Community Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your MongoDB data. This step upgrades the operator and verifies the installation.

```bash

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb -o yaml > mongodb-backup.yaml

helm upgrade mongodb-community-operator mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator --namespace mongodb --version 0.13.0

kubectl get pods -n mongodb

```

> Backs up the current MongoDB configuration.
> Upgrades the MongoDB Community Operator.
> Verifies that the operator pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb -o yaml > mongodb-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRD'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definition (CRD) may not be installed or may be outdated.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator/v0.13.0/deploy/crds/mongodb.com_mongodb_crd.yaml' to install the CRD.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The MongoDB pod may be failing due to misconfiguration or insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n mongodb' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: MongoDB resource is not ready
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The MongoDB instance may be stuck in a pending state due to insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster has enough resources and check the events with 'kubectl describe mongodb my-mongo -n mongodb'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to MongoDB'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The MongoDB service may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration with 'kubectl get svc -n mongodb' and ensure the service is of type 'ClusterIP' or 'LoadBalancer'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for MongoDB 5.0.3, improved Helm chart configurations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.11 or earlier, follow the migration guide at https://github.com/mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes/blob/master/docs/migration/community-operator-migration.md.

resolution.summary: You should see the MongoDB instance running with the command 'kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb' showing the status as 'Ready'.
codeSnippets:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 5.0.3
  storage:
    type: Durable
    size: 5Gi
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
